reviews.review_title: Developers have not updated in awhile.
reviews.review_description: great game and I still recommend it. However, I used to belong to a lot of the community groups, and watched in discord, read their road maps, and responses to others asking when features would be coming. Not a lot of action has been going on here for us. I suspect they are focusing on the mental health and wellness product to professionals. I am a therapist and I can see this use. However, I am disappointed to see this public VR product doesn't seem to be the focus any longer. I want to meditate with my spouse and was looking forward to news on multiplayer mode, which they responded would be challenging. But there hasn't been a peep more on discord. Also just ongoing development of the features, lessons, and more are kind of stalled to a couple of years ago. Not saying you shouldn 't buy this, just saying don't expect a really interested, and active development for us following a nice, busy roadmap into the future.

reviews.review_title: If you want to learn real meditation
reviews.review_description: Then this app is fantastic. Do check it out if you are tired of all the gamified nonsense meditation apps out there. This is more "hardcore" containing great lessons and everything without all of the distractions.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the review! Teaching authentic meditation is exactly what we aim for! We actually just pushed a new Alpha feature and I'd love to hear your feedback if you check it out and wanna drop a note in our discord. Happy meditating :)

reviews.review_description: I’ve tried a lot of apps to learn to meditate. This is the first that’s working for me.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the great review, it's always extremely heartening to hear that ZenVR is making a difference in people's lives!

reviews.review_title: My favorite meditation app to date
reviews.review_description: The app  is quite good as it is right now. It is a nice virtual meditation center (I’ve looked around). If the developers are interested in suggestions, I would suggest making it possible to pick the spot on the “grounds” for silent meditation and omit the meditation instructions currently in place for the silent meditation. Also, continuing to add additional dharma talks would be nice. Good luck on this project!

developer_response.body: Hi! Thank you for the kind words and great suggestions! We are continually developing new features and content for ZenVR and I'll pass these ideas along to the dev team! Happy meditating!

reviews.review_description:
There’s a lot I love about this app. The actual meditations, the sounds of birds and nature the studio.  I love the outdoors deck with the pagoda, cherry blossoms stream birds and swan. 

What I don’t care for is the teacher’s white eyes. They are creepy and it cuts right through you like the walking dead. The opposite of relaxing.  I can’t sit in the class and look at him. 

I’ve managed to put my self outside the door and enjoy the nature views. His voice is calming tho so that’s a plus. 

What I’d love is more meditation views like outside that has the Asian feel. That’s what makes this app so unique. Even if it’s a separate sit by yourself meditation option in main menu with option for music or nature sounds. Love the rain storm. Maybe a light snow storm or thunder and lightning would be great too. 
I love everything about this app other than his eyes.

This is a review of first experience and first lesson at the moment. I will try to update this once I experience the app more. 
So far, I do not find many positives to this app, it does look like it has been in the lab for over a year now, so you would think they would get it out to the real store by now, but there are definitely some things that I did not enjoy. 
The very beginning, I'm not sure why there's a need for account information, just grab what Quest account that is using it. Then, the movement is very awkward, and somehow made me appear below the ground level so my view was literally of the floor. The whole introduction honestly was a bit annoying and seemed unnecessary. I'm not sure if the outside environment will be part of the future lessons, but it's an extra that doesn't contribute to the experience since it is not visually appealing and movement is very frustrating. The turning is not smooth like walking is, it has fixed positions for some reason. 
It also makes no sense why there's no hand tracking at all when hand positioning is important and meditating with controllers is not exactly an authentic experience. 

Anyway, there's a lot to improve on, especially with quest 3 being out now, maybe mixed reality would be a good idea as well. I do not like leaving bad reviews, and since this is a software, it can be improved, therefore I should definitely return and update my review as this app grows. I would say that this app should not cost the $20 it is asking for at the moment, this still feels like something that should have been free.
Thank you.

developer_response.body:
Hi! I'm sorry you had some frustrations with the app - I'd like to clarify and address a few points you raised for anyone that might see this in the future and for your next time using ZenVR:


-First off, you're totally right, we have been in AppLab for a little while now, however the choice to move to the main store is not ours, but rather up to Meta's internal team priorities - we'd love to make the jump ourselves and hope to do so soon!


-I'm not sure what you experienced that was asking for "account information" - as of right now ZenVR does not do that. There is an opt-in sign up for our mailing list on the main menu, but that's entirely optional to the experience. If you experienced something else, perhaps you can send us a screenshot on our Discord so we can look into it?


-If you experienced a height calibration issue, can you confirm that it was only occurring for ZenVR? We've noticed sometimes the Oculus Guardian, especially on Quest 1 devices, can behave in unexpected ways, but when you return to the Oculus home you should be able to tell if it was an app specific issue - and reset it in the Oculus settings.


-I'm sorry that you found the introduction a bit annoying - we've found users are joining us from a variety of different experience levels with meditation with varying expectations and it can be helpful to ease them into an understanding of the ZenVR program with some upfront explanation - we are actively working on a big upgrade to it soon - but more than that, you can always press the A button on your controller to pause and skip the introduction if you feel like you don't need it!


-For locomotion - we offer a few different options including teleportation and smooth locomotion! We've found that turning in 45 degree increments feels the most comfortable to most users, but you can swap to smooth turning from the settings on the main menu.


-Hand tracking is a function we are planning to add, however for stability and accessibility we've kept the controllers as the primary mode of interaction for now - and really it's only for menu interactions and movement. I do believe the lessons encourage you to rest the controllers on the ground or your lap when meditating so you can perform the proper hand mudras.


-Moving through the outside environment is not strictly necessary for the first lessons, however, many users have found joy in the freedom of exploration and discovery of the campus or simply sitting under the waterfall while listening to a lesson - you're more than welcome to sit in dojo and ignore that if that doesn't appeal to you :)


We do agree that ZenVR still has plenty of room to grow and improve! We're a small team working hard to bring the best, authentic meditation experience to our users and push periodic updates with improvements, new features, and new content to keep growing the app. However, the heart of ZenVR is in the content and teachings found in each lesson - developed with experts and proven to be effective through published research - I'd encourage you to keep working through the lessons and trying to apply the content in your everyday life to see the most benefit from the experience! I'm certain as you progress you'll find the value it offers far exceeds the cost - as many other users have mentioned here as well! Please feel free to join our Discord community and share any other thoughts and feedback and let us know if there are any other issues we can resolve!�

Peace and Safe Travels

reviews.review_description:
I just wanted to mention how impressed I am that someone had taken the time to actually teach formal meditation (as in sitting positions, have positions, technique, etc.).  The developer is immediately reachable and responsive on Discord, and I'm looking forward to accessing the coming new content.

I purchased this not because I wanted to buy one more meditation app; rather, I wanted *technique* so that I can meditation practice.

... Now if only I could integrate this with my Muse neurofeedback device to measure what is happening in my brainwaves while meditating --- *that* would be beyond cool.

reviews.review_title: Like having a Zen master in your home!
reviews.review_description: This is not just a 3d environment or experience. It's an actual set of classes teaching you meditation techniques. Your not just along for the ride as it does require you to engage and progress through the lessons and commit to improving. Not something I would have done if I had to sign up for a class, drive to a location, pay some large fees but here in vr in my own home I'm enjoying it!

I was lucky enough to have beta tested this app before it made its way onto the oculus store and I was impressed then, the official first launch version in its original iteration is a Great Leap Forward for what was already an impressive start. Don’t be put off by the basic graphics, it’s the content that shines here and it does exactly what it promises.

Firstly and most importantly this isn’t one of those experiences where you sit in a trippy environment listening to plinky plonky music and drift off, this is a proper classroom style mediation training course with a real instructor teaching you real meditation techniques, the future applications of this approach are mind blowing, you could learn anything with this approach. Each lesson is roughly 30 mins then once you have completed the lesson you can go back into the dojo in practice mode and build upon the techniques you’ve learned and develops them further over time. The teacher is great (if rendered a little creepily) and the environment and sound effects are all brilliantly realised to make you feel like you are there. Less than half way through the first lesson and I was already feeling calm and centred. If you want to learn about meditation and form a practice that fits in with your life, this is 100% the app to go for. I can’t wait to see how it develops and what future lessons we can look forward to. Well done to the devs, they have achieved exactly what they set out to achieve and what they promised to deliver.

reviews.review_title: Perfect Way to Relax & Let Go of Anxiety
reviews.review_description: I purchased this a couple weeks ago.  I have only had a chance to do the first lesson a couple times, but I did feel extremely calm and relaxed.  My brain goes a 100 mph and after the first lesson I was content with just sitting their with barely a thought in my mind.  Wow! It was peaceful.

I've tried meditation numerous times over the years with different apps and books. With ZenVR I finally feel as though I can do it. The instruction is simple, engaging and makes sense. It gives you so much more than pleasant background music and staring at a candle. Definitely recommend this app.

This hidden gem is well worth your time! Easy menu navigation, calm environment, and a super supportive development team. Love the updates that have come out, and I can’t wait for MORE!

Thanks for the great experiences so far!
This is a well put together experience for people who meditate or would like to get into meditation. Great Discord as well to discuss in a friendly environment. Keep up the good work Dev team.

I can't believe I haven't reviewed this app until now. I believe the last time I planned to, I heard there were some changes on the way so I decided to hold off on the review so I could talk about that as well. Well I'm finally here, and I must say, with the (semi-) recent update, they've made an already great app even better. I love the new standalone environments and exploration. New lessons continue to be added so you can perfect your meditation technique. I've not spent a lot of time yet with the rowing, but being able to meditate on the open water was amazing... Once I could get the rhythm of the row into my body that is. LOL I also really like the ability to be able to meditate on my own too, unguided, in many of the new locations/outlooks. This app is well worth the price of admission and continually getting better with each update.

developer_response.body: Hey, thank you so much for the great review! We actually just pushed a new Lantern Calligraphy feature - it's still in Alpha, but would love to hear your feedback on it if you check it out - you can drop a line in Discord :) Happy meditating!

reviews.review_title: Learn how to meditate outside VR
reviews.review_description: Beautiful visuals and great teachings. Would recommend for a peaceful and grounding experience that can teach meditation even outside of VR.

reviews.review_title: Been waiting for an app like this!
reviews.review_description:
ZenVR is an excellent use of VR technology.. I’ve been hoping something like this would come out since VR allows you to go anywhere!
This app transports you to a quaint dojo with a soft spoken instructor who guides you through several lessons on meditation techniques. The environment is very cozy and pleasant including extraneous sounds that just add to the immersion and promote a peaceful mood. I prefer to meditate with closed eyes so I really enjoy the sounds of birds chirping and creaking wood of the dojo. 
The developers plan to release additional lessons soon which will be an automatic upgrade for existing users. 
I would love to see them add more locations in the future, but for now, the dojo serves perfectly!

I truly believe if we’re gonna use technology, let’s use it in productive and supportive ways.  I got ZenVR with the purpose in mind of learning how to establish a routine that I can take on my own without depending on a particular place to have the regular sessions. Where I currently live (Canadá, Manitoba) there are not even zen monasteries around. I feel this is the best app for meditation out there. This app doesn’t intent to make you an app addict or to take you on to a dependent state where you need to continue using it to feel well. I actually think it’s the opposite🙌🏼 

The app seems to be designed in a way that you take the practice and fundamentals and you can extend the practice in your own day to day life without depending on it. This is a good and more ethical use of technology. 

I foresee learning all the necessary steps and then incorporating it well in my life so I don’t have to be in the headset all the time trying to practice something that I need to actually break free. 

I finished the first lessons and the results are evident. The master really explains the foundation we need to build upon and follows by practice which is a great to make sure we know what and why we are doing it. 

Thanks so much to the developers for such a great app!

This is by far the best meditation App I've ever come across. Other apps show you cool graphics and/or guide you through each meditation. However, you will be limited in that you would have to use these apps to meditate every time.

ZenVR is different. The creators reproduced the space and setting of how Meditation is taught in real life. In other words, you are attending a class to learn meditation from professionals at the comfort of your home and at an affordable price. With real curricula and meditation skills you can perform on your own any time.

ZenVR is not an app to depend on to meditate.
ZenVR teaches you to meditate independently.

Thank you for teaching me these valuable skills that imlrove my jealth and quality of life.

reviews.review_title: Content is good but I get a stiff neck
reviews.review_description: Meditation in VR is nothing for me, I get a stiff neck, it really hurts after some time. The content is good, but the graphic is really bad. At least the animation of the Teacher should be better. Hands moving through the body and all the unnatural movement are breaking the immersion.

reviews.review_title: Best Way to Truly Learn to Meditate
reviews.review_description:
I have tried just about every meditation app so far in the Oculus store. None of them have ever taught me how to truly meditate on my own except for Zenvr. This is a great experience especially for beginners. The teacher in the classroom dojo walks you through everything from beginner to advanced. The environment is very peaceful and relaxing. 
 Another great thing is you can practice anytime you want to increase your meditation skills. This is a great idea the developers created.This meditation school has really helped me to understand more about the health benefits of mediation. Also it has helped me to start applying what I have learned to everyday life.

  Thank you Zenvr for creating such a great meditation experience. Also in the future a lie down or recline mode would make this app amazing!
